title: Update .categories.json with superpowers/* and obsidian/* qualified names
description: After code changes: add superpowers category with 14 qualified names (superpowers/brainstorming etc.) and obsidian category with 5 qualified names. Run instill pick-skills or show-library to confirm exact names first.
close_reason: Added superpowers (14 skills) and obsidian (5 skills) categories with qualified names to .categories.json

title: Add bats integration tests for group-dir skills
description: Add make_group_skill helper and bats tests for: pick-skills adds qualified group skill and creates nested symlink; removing it prunes empty parent; two siblings in same group where removing one keeps the parent.
close_reason: 3 bats tests added: add qualified skill, remove prunes parent, sibling preservation; all 21 pass

title: Update reconcile to create/remove nested symlinks for qualified names
description: reconcile.go computes source as filepath.Join(libraryPath, name) and listExistingSymlinks only returns top-level symlink names. Fix: (1) replace source computation with SkillSourcePath call; (2) ensureRealDirectory for parent before creating nested symlinks; (3) listExistingSymlinks walks one level into real subdirs and returns qualified names; (4) removeSymlink prunes empty parent dirs. Tests: nested creation, parent pruning, sibling preservation.
close_reason: Reconcile creates nested symlinks for qualified names, prunes empty parent dirs, listExistingSymlinks returns qualified names; all 149 tests pass

title: Add SkillSourcePath and update ListLibrarySkills for group-dir discovery
description: library.go only scans one level deep. Add SkillSourcePath(libraryPath, name) that resolves: flat (library/<name>/SKILL.md) and group child (library/<group>/<name>/SKILL.md when name contains one /). Update ListLibrarySkills: for top-level dirs without SKILL.md, scan one level deeper for child dirs with SKILL.md and emit group/child qualified names. Update SkillExists to call SkillSourcePath. Create library_test.go.
close_reason: SkillSourcePath added; ListLibrarySkills now discovers group-dir children; 8 new tests passing

title: Relax IsValidSkillName to allow one slash for qualified skill names
description: IsValidSkillName in manifest.go explicitly blocks / in names. Relax to allow exactly one slash so qualified names like superpowers/brainstorming are valid. Each segment must still pass individual safety checks (not empty, not ., not .., no backslash, filepath.Clean == self). Tests: reject a/b/c, //foo, /foo, foo/, ../x; accept superpowers/brainstorming.
close_reason: IsValidSkillName relaxed to allow one slash; tests added and passing

title: end-to-end permission reconciliation coverage and docs
description: Add CLI/Bats coverage and documentation updates for automatic Claude skill permissions.
acceptance_criteria: Bats verifies pick/add then check-skills grants permission and remove then check-skills revokes it; README/llms docs describe settings.local.json behavior; full quality gates pass.
notes:
Review summary for instill-n0p.3:

/golang-cli: Initial review found README overclaimed check-skills revocation and Bats wording implied check-skills performed the revoke after pick-skills had already reconciled. Resolved by documenting that check-skills grants from the current manifest, while pick-skills/selection flows revoke using previous-manifest ownership; Bats test renamed and tightened to assert pick-skills revocation directly.

/golang-code-style: Found a long README build badge line. Resolved by converting the build badge to reference-style Markdown. Line-length scan over README.md, llms.txt, and test/instill.bats found no lines over 120 chars.

/golang-security: No findings. Docs identify settings.local.json as local/gitignored and do not overpromise security boundaries after the wording fix.

/golang-testing: No findings. Focused Bats coverage verifies grant and selection-driven revoke without jq and also checks .gitignore includes .claude/settings.local.json.

Conflict:
The original Bats wording could be read as requiring check-skills to revoke arbitrary manual manifest edits, but the implemented safe ownership model only revokes when a selection flow provides the previous manifest.

Decision:
Document and test the selection-driven revoke contract, while documenting check-skills as grant/current-manifest reconciliation.

Rationale:
Without stored historical ownership state, check-skills cannot safely distinguish a manually removed manifest skill from a manual local permission. Selection flows have previous-manifest context and can revoke deterministically.

Verification: focused Bats filter for permission grant/revoke and init-project gitignore passed; line-length scan over README.md, llms.txt, and test/instill.bats passed.
close_reason: Added focused Bats permission grant/revoke coverage and documentation, resolved review wording conflict, and verified focused Bats plus docs line lengths.

title: reconcile Skill permissions from manifest ownership
description: Wire ReconcileManifest so finalSkills grant Skill(name) permissions and removed manifest-owned skills revoke only the matching managed Skill(name) entries.
acceptance_criteria: Grant final skills; revoke pre-reconcile manifest skills absent from finalSkills; leave manually added Skill entries untouched; produce no stdout for permission-only changes.
notes:
Review summary for instill-n0p.2:

/golang-cli: No findings on initial review. Second-pass review after fixes: No findings. Permission-only reconciliation remains silent and CLI-visible output is unchanged.

/golang-code-style: Initial review found long inline nolint/comment and a sameStrings helper duplicating slices.Equal. Resolved by moving the nolint to its own line, wrapping comments, using slices.Equal, and removing sameStrings. Second-pass review requested replacing reflect.DeepEqual in tests with slices.Equal/bytes.Equal; resolved and verified no reflect.DeepEqual remains.

/golang-security: Initial review found three blockers: symlinked .claude/.claude/skills could escape project-local symlink reconciliation, post-update callers lacked previous ownership state for revocation, and malformed settings.local.json shapes were rewritten instead of failing closed. Resolved by validating real .claude and skills directories before symlink mutation, adding ReconcileManifestWithPrevious and using it from PickSkills/ApplySkillSelection, and rejecting malformed permissions shapes. Second-pass review found manifest partial-update risk when settings validation failed after WriteManifestAtomic. Resolved by preflighting reconcile directories and settings shape before manifest writes in selection flows.

/golang-testing: Initial review: No findings. Second-pass review requested malformed-settings failure coverage at PickSkills level and direct settings.local.json symlink coverage. Resolved with PickSkills and ApplySkillSelection malformed-settings tests that assert manifest unchanged, plus direct settings.local symlink rejection coverage.

Conflict:
One path was to let ReconcileManifest infer previous ownership from the manifest argument for all callers. Another was to introduce an explicit previous-manifest API for callers that already changed the desired skill set.

Decision:
Keep ReconcileManifest for unchanged check-skills callers, and add ReconcileManifestWithPrevious for PickSkills and ApplySkillSelection so permission revocation uses the real pre-change ownership set.

Rationale:
check-skills reads the current manifest as both previous and desired state, but selection flows compute a new desired state from an older manifest. Explicit previous ownership prevents removed skills from becoming accidental manual permissions.

Conflict:
Malformed settings.local.json could be repaired best-effort or rejected before mutation.

Decision:
Reject malformed permissions shapes and preflight before manifest writes in selection flows.

Rationale:
Failing closed preserves local permission data and prevents partial manifest updates that would make later revocation impossible.

Verification: go test ./internal/instill -run 'Test(Reconcile|PickSkills|ApplySkillSelection)' -count=1 passed; line-length scan over touched Go files found no lines over 120 chars; rg found no reflect.DeepEqual or sameStrings in internal/instill.
close_reason: Implemented ReconcileManifest permission wiring, resolved security/style/testing findings, documented conflicts, and verified focused reconcile/selection tests.

title: settings.local.json permission model helpers
description: Build and test the JSON read/write helpers for .claude/settings.local.json so permissions.allow can be created, initialized, preserved, and written atomically.
acceptance_criteria: Helpers create missing settings.local.json; initialize missing permissions.allow; preserve unrelated JSON fields and manual permission entries; use existing atomic write pattern.
notes:
Review summary for instill-n0p.1:

/golang-cli: No findings on initial review. Re-review after helper contract change: No findings. Helper remains silent and usable from ReconcileManifest.

/golang-code-style: Initial review: No findings. Re-review found two P3 long assertion lines. Resolved by splitting the assertions at semantic boundaries; line-length scan found no lines over 120 chars in settings_local files.

/golang-security: Initial review found P1 risk that replacement-style permissions.allow writes could erase manual local permissions. Resolved by changing the helper contract to reconcile previousSkills/finalSkills and preserve manual/non-owned entries. Re-review found P2 symlinked .claude directory escape risk before atomic writes. Resolved with Lstat validation of the .claude directory before writing plus regression coverage.

/golang-testing: Initial review found P2 no-op test weakness and P3 missing existing-settings-without-permissions coverage. Resolved with modtime stability assertion for true no-write behavior and an existing settings file test that creates permissions while preserving top-level fields. Re-review: No findings.

Conflict:
Helper contract could either replace permissions.allow with a caller-supplied list, or reconcile permissions using explicit previous/final manifest ownership inputs.

Decision:
Use reconcileSettingsLocalPermissions(path, previousSkills, finalSkills) so the helper removes only previously managed Skill entries absent from finalSkills, preserves manual entries, and appends missing final Skill permissions.

Rationale:
The parent New Spec and ADR make the Manifest the ownership boundary. Replacement is simpler but can delete user-local permissions; explicit reconciliation is safer and auditable.

Verification: go test ./internal/instill -run 'TestReconcileSettingsLocalPermissions' -count=1 passed; line-length scan over settings_local.go/settings_local_test.go found no lines over 120 chars.
close_reason: Implemented settings.local.json permission reconciliation helpers, resolved review findings, documented contract conflict, and verified focused helper tests.

title: reconcile: grant and revoke Claude agent permissions in settings.local.json
description:
## What to build

When `instill check-skills` (or any call to `ReconcileManifest`) runs, it should reconcile `Skill(name)` entries in `.claude/settings.local.json` to match the post-reconcile manifest. This means:

- **Grant**: add `"Skill(name)"` to `permissions.allow` for each skill in `finalSkills` that doesn't already have an entry
- **Revoke**: remove `"Skill(name)"` from `permissions.allow` for each skill that was in the pre-reconcile manifest but is absent from `finalSkills`
- **Leave untouched**: any `Skill(name)` entry whose name was never in the manifest (manually added by the developer)

The manifest is the ownership boundary — instill only manages permissions for skills it also manages as symlinks (see ADR `docs/adr/0001-manifest-as-permission-ownership-boundary.md`).

**File handling**: create `settings.local.json` if absent; initialize `permissions.allow` as an empty array if the key is missing. All writes are atomic (write to `.tmp`, rename).

**Output**: silent — no stdout output for permission changes.

**Skill name format**: `"Skill(name)"` where name is the raw manifest skill name (e.g. `"Skill(golang-testing)"`, `"Skill(superpowers:tdd)"`).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Running `instill check-skills` after adding a skill to the manifest creates a `"Skill(name)"` entry in `.claude/settings.local.json`
- [ ] Running `instill check-skills` after removing a skill from the manifest removes the corresponding `"Skill(name)"` entry
- [ ] A `Skill(name)` entry added manually (not via instill) is not removed during reconcile
- [ ] `settings.local.json` is created if it does not exist
- [ ] `permissions.allow` is initialized if absent from an existing `settings.local.json`
- [ ] All writes are atomic
- [ ] No stdout output is produced for permission changes
- [ ] Unit tests cover grant, revoke, leave-untouched, create-from-scratch, and no-op paths
- [ ] Bats integration test verifies end-to-end: pick a skill, run check-skills, assert entry present; remove skill, run check-skills, assert entry gone

## Blocked by

- instill-ciq (init-project: add .claude/settings.local.json to .gitignore)
notes:
Parent completion summary for instill-n0p:

Task type: New Spec. Source of truth was Beads. Broken into child tasks instill-n0p.1, instill-n0p.2, and instill-n0p.3, all closed with required /golang-cli, /golang-code-style, /golang-security, and /golang-testing review summaries.

Completed behavior:
- init-project gitignores .claude/settings.local.json.
- settings.local.json permission reconciliation grants Skill(name) for final manifest skills.
- selection flows revoke removed managed Skill(name) permissions using previous-manifest ownership.
- manual Skill(...) permissions outside manifest ownership are preserved.
- malformed settings permissions fail closed before selection flows mutate the manifest.
- symlinked .claude, .claude/skills, and settings.local.json paths are rejected before writes escape the project boundary.
- docs and Bats clarify check-skills grants/current-manifest reconciliation versus selection-driven revokes.

Final verification so far:
- go test ./... passed.
- make bats-test passed all 18 tests.
- go vet ./... passed.
- make lint passed with 0 issues.

Conflict:
The original request phrased revoke verification around check-skills, but check-skills lacks historical ownership state for arbitrary manual manifest edits.

Decision:
Implement and document revocation through PickSkills/ApplySkillSelection, where previous-manifest ownership is available; keep check-skills as current-manifest grant/reconcile and library-prune behavior.

Rationale:
This preserves the manifest ownership boundary without treating unknown/manual local permissions as instill-managed state.
close_reason: Implemented default Claude Skill permission reconciliation with docs, Bats coverage, four-lane reviews per child task, and full quality gates passing.

title: init-project: add .claude/settings.local.json to .gitignore
description:
## What to build

When `instill init-project` runs, it should add `.claude/settings.local.json` to the project's `.gitignore` alongside the existing `.claude/skills/` entry. This prevents developers from accidentally committing their local agent permissions.

The change mirrors the existing `ensureGitignoreEntry` pattern in `init_project.go`: check if the entry is already present, append it if not, report the change to stdout, no-op if already there.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `instill init-project` appends `.claude/settings.local.json` to `.gitignore` when not already present
- [ ] Output reports `updated: .gitignore (+.claude/settings.local.json)` when added
- [ ] No-op (no output, no write) when entry already exists
- [ ] Unit test covers both the add and no-op paths
- [ ] Existing `.claude/skills/` gitignore behavior is unchanged

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
notes:
Review summary for instill-ciq:

/golang-cli: No findings. Verified the CLI-visible behavior is compatible with existing init-project stdout conventions and keeps diagnostic output separate.

/golang-code-style: Found P3 signature style issue in ensureGitignoreEntry(root string, entry string). Resolved by grouping adjacent string parameters as ensureGitignoreEntry(root, entry string). Re-review not required because change is mechanical and gofmt/focused tests pass.

/golang-security: No findings. Existing symlinked .gitignore refusal and project-root-scoped file access are preserved.

/golang-testing: No findings. Unit tests cover adding .claude/settings.local.json, no-op/no-write when present, and preservation of .claude/skills/ behavior.

Conflict resolution: No conflicts detected among review lanes.

Verification: go test ./internal/instill -run 'TestInitProject.*Gitignore|TestInitProjectCreatesManifestSkillsDirAndGitignore' -count=1 passed.
close_reason: Implemented .claude/settings.local.json gitignore entry, recorded four review lanes, resolved style finding, and verified focused init-project tests.

title: Global search mode in pick-skills TUI
description:
Why: Users who know what skill they want should not be forced to browse the category tree. A global search mode provides instant access across all skills.

What: Pressing / in the two-pane TUI switches to a flat search mode: the two-pane layout is replaced by a single filterable list of all skills (matching the current TUI behaviour). The search query is shown in a prompt line. Pressing Escape exits search mode and returns to the two-pane browse view at the previously selected category. Pressing Enter in search mode confirms the selection and exits the TUI.
notes:
Review ledger for instill-mx6 global search mode:

/golang-cli: APPROVED. Verified TUI command semantics: / enters a flat all-skill search mode, browse panes are replaced by one list, Escape returns to the previous browse category without cancellation, and Enter confirms. A scripted PTY run confirmed global matches outside the current category and manifest write on confirmation.

/golang-code-style: APPROVED. searchMode/searchCursor and searchPaneLines fit the existing unexported Bubbletea model style; naming and tests are idiomatic; gofmt and diff checks are clean.

/golang-design-patterns: APPROVED. Browse state and search state are separated cleanly. visibleSkills bypasses category scope only in search mode, while browse mode preserves visibleCategorySkills. Escape clears query/search cursor while preserving browse state. Tradeoff noted: j/k are query text in search mode, so search navigation is arrow-key based.

/golang-linter: APPROVED. git diff --check, go test, go vet, go build, and make lint all passed with no findings.

/golang-safety: APPROVED. Verified cancellation semantics, independent search/browse cursor bounds, no-match behavior, hidden selection stability, and preserved browse state after Escape.

/golang-testing: APPROVED. Tests prove / enters flat global search from a drilled category, prompt line displays the query, two-pane layout is hidden while searching, Escape restores the previous browse category without cancelling, and existing search toggle/Enter behavior still works.

Conflict resolution: No blocking conflicts. The /golang-design-patterns note about j/k is a deliberate text-entry tradeoff rather than conflicting feedback. Chosen path: keep j/k as query characters in search mode and use arrow keys for search navigation. Rationale: search mode is explicitly a text-entry state, and this matches the pre-existing filter behavior while avoiding ambiguous interpretation of typed query characters.

Verification: go test ./internal/instill -run TestSkillPicker -count=1 passed; go test ./... passed; make lint passed with 0 issues. Reviewers additionally ran go test -race ./..., go vet ./..., go build ./..., diff checks, and scripted PTY checks.
close_reason: Implemented flat global search mode, recorded all six review lanes, resolved tradeoff rationale, and verified focused/full tests plus lint.

title: Drill-down subcategory navigation in TUI left pane
description:
Why: Categories like cloud/azure have subcategories. The left pane needs to support navigating into them without flattening the hierarchy into a long list.

What: Extend the left pane to support drill-down navigation. Right arrow on a category that has subcategories replaces the left pane list with those subcategories. Left arrow navigates up one level. A breadcrumb line above the left pane shows the current path (e.g. cloud > azure). Top-level categories are always reachable by pressing left arrow until the breadcrumb is empty.
notes:
Review ledger for instill-yox drill-down TUI navigation:

/golang-cli: APPROVED. Verified right arrow drills into categories with children, left arrow walks back up, breadcrumbs render drilled path, and a live PTY run exercised nested navigation. No requested changes.

/golang-code-style: APPROVED. gofmt-clean, idiomatic local model/helper style, no style findings. Verified go test, go test -race, go vet, make lint, and diff checks. No requested changes.

/golang-design-patterns: APPROVED. Accepted explicit categoryPath state plus helper methods for selected path, child discovery, scoped categories, and breadcrumb rendering. Noted future global search should be an explicit mode because browse filtering is category scoped. No requested changes for this task.

/golang-linter: APPROVED. make lint reported 0 issues; build, tests, vet, and diff checks passed. No requested changes.

/golang-safety: APPROVED. Verified empty child lists, cursor reset/clamp, All behavior, repeated left-to-root, leaf right-arrow behavior, and hidden selections remaining stable. No requested changes.

/golang-testing: CHANGES REQUESTED initially. Missing regression coverage proving text filtering remains scoped after drilling into a subcategory. Implemented TestSkillPickerFilterStaysScopedAfterDrilldown, which drills into cloud/azure, applies /azure, and verifies matches outside that drilled category stay hidden. Re-review APPROVED with no remaining blockers.

Conflict resolution: No reviewer feedback conflicted. The design-pattern note about future global search aligns with the next bead rather than changing this task. The only requested change was additive test coverage, so the chosen path was to preserve current behavior and add a focused regression test. Rationale: this keeps drill-down browsing deterministic and prepares the next task to introduce global search as a separate explicit mode.

Verification after resolved finding: go test ./internal/instill -run TestSkillPicker -count=1 passed; go test ./... passed; make lint passed with 0 issues.
close_reason: Implemented drill-down category navigation, recorded all six review lanes, resolved testing coverage finding, and verified focused/full tests plus lint.

title: Right pane filters by selected category
description:
Why: The two panes need to be integrated — selecting a category must narrow the skill list to make browsing useful.

What: When a category is selected in the left pane, the right pane updates to show only skills assigned that category (including subcategory prefix matches). Selecting 'All' (or any state when registry is absent) shows all skills. The right pane resets its scroll position when the category selection changes.
notes:
Review closure for right-pane category filtering:

/golang-cli initially requested restoring an All category when a registry exists. Implemented categoryPaneEntries to always prepend All, so the initial view remains unfiltered and users can return to all skills. Re-review approved.

/golang-code-style raised the same missing All issue, then approved after All became explicit and tests covered the normal registry path.

/golang-design-patterns raised the same missing All sentinel as a compatibility problem for search and browse. Chosen path: keep All as a picker-local sentinel prepended before sorted registry categories. Re-review approved; noted the string sentinel is acceptable because it is localized and tested.

/golang-linter initially requested resolving staticcheck QF1001 in the category-order assertion. Rewrote the assertion to direct index comparisons; make lint returned 0 issues and re-review approved.

/golang-safety approved: All fallback, hidden selection retention, cursor reset/clamping, and exact-or-selectedCategory-slash prefix boundaries are safe.

/golang-testing initially requested the normal registry All path. Added tests asserting All,cloud,golang ordering, all skills visible initially, moving to cloud filters to cloud plus subcategories, and category movement resets the skill cursor. Re-review approved.

Conflict resolution:
- Five reviewers converged on the same issue: All must exist even when categories are present. Chosen path: always prepend All rather than making it only a fallback. This preserves the full-list initial state and provides an explicit escape hatch from category filters.
- Linter cleanup was non-conflicting and applied exactly.

Verification after resolved findings:
- go test ./internal/instill -run TestSkillPicker passed (13 tests)
- go test ./... passed (90 tests)
- make lint passed with 0 issues
- all six required review lanes approved after fixes: /golang-cli, /golang-code-style, /golang-design-patterns, /golang-linter, /golang-safety, /golang-testing

Downstream notes:
- instill-yox should build on the existing All sentinel and categoryCursor model when adding drilldown path state.
- instill-mx6 should preserve All/global search semantics and avoid starting scoped to the first category.
close_reason: Implemented category-scoped right pane filtering with All sentinel, subcategory prefix matches, cursor reset, tests, six-lane review notes, and verification.

title: Left pane wired to categories registry
description:
Why: The scaffold has a placeholder left pane. This slice connects it to real category data from .categories.json.

What: Replace the hardcoded left pane content in RunPickSkillsTUI with top-level categories loaded from LoadCategories. When the registry is absent or returns no categories, the left pane shows a single 'All' entry and the right pane shows all skills (preserving current behaviour). When categories are present, the left pane lists them alphabetically. Selecting a category does not yet filter the right pane — that is the next slice.
notes:
Review closure for left pane category registry wiring:

/golang-cli approved: TUI loads category entries from the library registry, derives top-level category names alphabetically, falls back to All when no category entries exist, and keeps the right pane unfiltered because visibleSkills still only applies the text filter.

/golang-code-style approved: deterministic sorting, copy-returning helpers, existing normalization conventions, and focused tests match local Go style.

/golang-design-patterns approved with tradeoffs: silent TUI fallback is appropriate to avoid corrupting Bubbletea output; malformed registries appear as no categories in the TUI and can be diagnosed later via doctor; filtering slice will need the full category map or an index; drilldown remains compatible because full paths are preserved in the registry loader.

/golang-linter approved: go test, make build, make lint, make vet, and git diff --check passed.

/golang-safety approved: absent/malformed registry silently falls back, empty registry becomes All, nested paths reduce to sorted top-level categories, category selection does not filter yet, and category focus cannot mutate skills.

/golang-testing initially requested two missing tests: category navigation must not filter skills yet, and an empty valid registry must fall back to All. Implemented TestSkillPickerCategoryNavigationDoesNotFilterSkillsYet and TestSkillPickerCategoriesForLibraryEmptyRegistryFallsBackToAll; re-review approved.

Conflict resolution:
- No implementation conflict was raised. The only change request was additional test coverage. Chosen path: add focused model tests at the category-pane boundary rather than forcing an integration TTY test, keeping the behavior deterministic and fast.

Verification after resolved findings:
- go test ./internal/instill -run TestSkillPicker passed (12 tests)
- go test ./... passed (89 tests)
- /golang-cli, /golang-code-style, /golang-design-patterns, /golang-linter, /golang-safety, and /golang-testing all approved after the test additions.

Downstream notes for instill-19u:
- Model currently carries only category labels. Filtering will need to carry the category map or a category-to-skills index and update visibleSkills to combine selected category with text search.
- Preserve All fallback and reset the right-pane cursor when the selected category changes.
close_reason: Wired pick-skills left pane to category registry top-level entries with All fallback, preserved unfiltered right pane behavior, added tests, recorded six-lane review notes, and verified Go tests/lint.

title: instill doctor uncategorized skills warning
description:
Why: As the library grows, newly added skills will silently have no category. A doctor warning surfaces this so users know to update .categories.json.

What: Extend the existing reconcile/check flow to load .categories.json and compare against all library skills. Print uncategorized: <skill-name> for each skill with no category assignment. Silent (no output, no error) when .categories.json is absent — this is not an error state. Only warns when the registry exists but a skill is missing from it.
notes:
Review closure for check-skills uncategorized warnings:

/golang-cli approved: absent registry exits 0 with empty stdout/stderr, malformed registry exits 0 with parse warning and no uncategorized spam, partial registry reports uncategorized docker and omits categorized golang-cli.

/golang-code-style approved: CategoryRegistryExists, LoadCategoriesStrict, warnUncategorizedSkills, and tests are idiomatic and follow local writer/error patterns.

/golang-design-patterns initially requested distinguishing malformed registries from valid-empty registries. Implemented LoadCategoriesStrict and changed check-skills to emit a parse warning then skip uncategorized output on strict-load failure. Re-review approved.

/golang-linter approved: go test, go build, go vet, golangci-lint v2.6.2, focused tests, and diff checks passed.

/golang-safety initially requested avoiding false uncategorized warnings for malformed .categories.json. Implemented strict loading; malformed registries now warn on stderr and return before emitting uncategorized lines. Re-review approved.

/golang-testing initially requested exact silence when registry is absent with no reconcile changes. Updated the missing-registry test to precreate the symlink and assert stdout/stderr are exactly empty; added malformed-registry regression coverage. Re-review approved.

Conflict resolution:
- The task only specified silence when the registry is absent, but malformed present registries cannot safely support missing-skill analysis. Chosen path: use strict loading in check-skills only; present malformed registries produce a parse warning and no uncategorized false positives, while absent registries remain silent.
- Reconcile still runs before category diagnostics so existing symlink/manifest behavior remains primary.

Verification after resolved findings:
- go test ./internal/cli -run TestCheckSkills passed (8 tests)
- go test ./... passed (97 tests)
- go test -race ./... passed per design-pattern re-review
- make test passed including 17 BATS tests per design-pattern re-review
- all six required lanes approved after fixes: /golang-cli, /golang-code-style, /golang-design-patterns, /golang-linter, /golang-safety, /golang-testing

Downstream note for instill-rhi:
- Use LoadCategoriesStrict or an equivalent stateful loader when categorize needs to distinguish absent, invalid, and valid existing registries.
close_reason: Added check-skills uncategorized warnings gated on valid present category registry, strict malformed-registry handling, exact silence tests, six-lane review notes, and verification.

title: show-library --category filter flag
description:
Why: Users need a quick CLI way to browse skills by category without launching the TUI.

What: Add --category <path> flag to instill show-library. Loads .categories.json via LoadCategories. Filters the skill list to those assigned the given category path — including prefix matches so --category cloud also matches cloud/azure skills. Output format is unchanged (alphabetical list with [✓]/[ ] markers). Degrades silently to unfiltered output when registry is absent.
notes:
Review closure for show-library --category:

/golang-cli approved: --category is wired through Cobra into ShowLibraryOptions; ShowLibrary applies category filtering before the existing formatter; help exposes --category; manual probes confirmed category and absent-registry behavior.

/golang-code-style approved: flag plumbing follows the existing --filter/options pattern; core filtering preserves output ordering and selected count conventions.

/golang-design-patterns approved with tradeoff: FilterSkillsByCategory is small and uses exact-or-prefix matching, but the shared matcher currently lives in skill_picker.go. Chosen path: keep this non-blocking for this slice and consider moving the matcher to categories.go during later cleanup. Silent malformed-registry fallback is intentionally consistent with the TUI path.

/golang-linter approved: go test, go build, go vet, golangci-lint v2.6.2, gofmt, and smoke tests passed.

/golang-safety approved: absent/malformed registry degrades to unfiltered output, prefix matching uses exact or category slash boundaries, selected count counts visible filtered skills, write errors are propagated, and manifest reconciliation still happens before listing.

/golang-testing initially requested CLI-level coverage proving missing .categories.json is silent. Added TestShowLibraryCLIWithCategoryAndMissingRegistryIsSilent asserting exit 0, exact unfiltered stdout, and empty stderr. Re-review approved.

Conflict resolution:
- No behavior conflict. The only requested change was stronger CLI coverage for silent fallback; implemented as a focused CLI test.
- Design noted the category matcher location is awkward but not blocking. Chosen path: defer relocation unless a later task creates duplication or clearer ownership pressure.

Verification after resolved findings:
- go test ./internal/instill -run TestShowLibrary passed (7 tests)
- go test ./internal/cli -run TestShowLibraryCLIWithCategory passed (2 tests)
- go test ./... passed (94 tests)
- /golang-cli, /golang-code-style, /golang-design-patterns, /golang-linter, /golang-safety, and /golang-testing all approved.
close_reason: Added show-library --category filtering with category-prefix matches, silent missing-registry fallback, CLI/core tests, six-lane review notes, and verification.

title: instill categorize auto-classify command
description:
Why: Populating .categories.json for 187 existing skills manually would take hours. Auto-classification from name prefixes covers ~70% of skills instantly, leaving only ambiguous ones for manual review.

What: New instill categorize subcommand. Reads all skill names from the library. Applies prefix rules to assign categories (e.g. golang-* → golang, azure-* → cloud/azure, dd-* → datadog, k8s-* and docker → cloud). Writes a draft .categories.json to the library root. Idempotent — skips skills already present in the registry. Prints uncategorized: <name> for skills it cannot classify. Safe to re-run as the library grows.
notes:
Review closure for instill categorize command:

/golang-cli approved: categorize is wired as a no-arg Cobra subcommand, resolves the configured library, classifies golang- to golang, azure- to cloud/azure, dd- to datadog, k8s- and docker to cloud, preserves existing assignments, prints uncategorized for unknowns, bootstraps missing registries, and exits 1 without rewriting malformed registries.

/golang-code-style initially requested avoiding the forgiving loader in a write path. Switched CategorizeLibrary to use CategoryRegistryExists plus LoadCategoriesStrict when .categories.json exists. Re-review approved.

/golang-design-patterns initially requested fail-closed behavior for malformed registries. Implemented strict load before classification/write and added regression coverage. Re-review approved.

/golang-linter initially raised the same malformed-registry overwrite risk despite lint passing. After strict-load fix, re-review approved with go test, go vet, go build, and golangci-lint v2.6.2 clean.

/golang-safety initially requested preventing malformed registry overwrite and manual assignment loss. Implemented strict loading and tests proving malformed contents are preserved. Re-review approved.

/golang-testing approved coverage for prefix rules, existing registry preservation, idempotency, CLI wiring, uncategorized output, and malformed registry rejection/preservation.

Conflict resolution:
- All change requests converged on one issue: a writer command must not use a forgiving read API. Chosen path: tolerate missing registry as bootstrap, but use LoadCategoriesStrict when a registry exists and return ExitGeneral before any write if it cannot be parsed.
- Prefix rules remain code-based rather than table-configurable for this slice because the Bead specifies fixed examples and current scope is small.

Verification after resolved findings:
- go test ./internal/instill -run TestCategorize passed (4 tests)
- go test ./internal/cli -run TestCategorize passed (2 tests)
- go test ./... passed (103 tests)
- make lint passed with 0 issues
- all six required lanes approved after fixes: /golang-cli, /golang-code-style, /golang-design-patterns, /golang-linter, /golang-safety, /golang-testing
close_reason: Added instill categorize command with prefix classification, existing assignment preservation, fail-closed malformed registry handling, idempotency tests, six-lane review notes, and verification.

title: Two-pane Bubbletea scaffold
description:
Why: The two-pane layout is the structural foundation for the new pick-skills TUI. Starting with a hardcoded scaffold means we get a compilable, runnable baseline before wiring real data.

What: Rewrite RunPickSkillsTUI in internal/instill to use a two-pane Bubbletea layout. Left pane shows a hardcoded placeholder list (e.g. ["(categories)"]). Right pane shows the existing flat skill list unchanged. Left/right arrow keys switch pane focus — left arrow focuses the categories pane, right arrow focuses the skills pane. Space selects/deselects skills in the right pane. Enter confirms and applies. The result must compile, run, and be functionally equivalent to the current TUI from the user's perspective (skill selection still works).
notes:
Review closure for two-pane Bubbletea scaffold:

/golang-cli approved: TUI still applies through ApplySkillSelection after confirmed Enter, default focus remains on the skills pane, the left pane has the hardcoded (categories) scaffold, left/right switch focus, and space toggles only in the skills pane.

/golang-code-style approved: unexported pane state and rendering helpers are idiomatic and scoped to the model; gofmt, go vet, make test, and make lint passed.

/golang-design-patterns approved with follow-forward notes for downstream tasks: registry wiring should expand newSkillPickerModel to accept category data; drilldown should add explicit browse path state; global search should likely become an explicit mode so Escape can return to browse instead of canceling; long category labels may need layout handling.

/golang-linter approved: go test, go vet, make lint, and git diff --check passed.

/golang-safety approved: empty visible skill lists render safely, selection is gated to the skills pane, focus changes are bounded, search clamps the skill cursor, and cancel paths avoid ApplySkillSelection.

/golang-testing approved: tests cover two-pane headers, placeholder category row, default skills-pane focus, left/right focus switching, blocking selection while categories are focused, plus existing filter/toggle/confirm/cancel/large-list behavior.

Conflict resolution:
- No reviewer conflicts were raised for this slice. Design-pattern follow-forward items are intentionally deferred to dependent beads because this bead is scoped to a hardcoded scaffold.

Verification after review:
- go test ./internal/instill -run TestSkillPicker|TestRunPickSkillsTUI|TestApplySkillSelection passed (10 tests)
- go test ./... passed
- make test passed including 17 BATS tests per reviewers
- make lint passed with 0 issues

Downstream notes:
- instill-1zo should replace the hardcoded categories slice with top-level category data from LoadCategoriesWithWarnings and keep All fallback behavior.
- instill-yox should introduce browse path state before repurposing right arrow for category drilldown.
- instill-mx6 should use an explicit global search mode rather than overloading the current filtering flag.
close_reason: Implemented two-pane Bubbletea scaffold with category placeholder pane, skill pane focus, left/right focus switching, right-pane selection behavior, tests, six-lane review notes, and verification.

title: Categories registry foundation
description:
Why: Everything in the hierarchy feature depends on being able to read .categories.json from the library root. This is the foundational data layer.

What: Implement LoadCategories(libraryPath string) in internal/instill that reads and parses <library_path>/.categories.json. The file format is category-first JSON: { "golang": ["golang-cli", "golang-testing"], "cloud/azure": ["azure-blob-storage"] }. Returns an empty map (not an error) when the file is absent or malformed, and prints a warning to stderr. Expose a CategoryForSkill(categories map, skillName string) helper that returns the category path for a given skill name (reverse lookup).
notes:
Review closure for categories registry foundation:

/golang-cli requested rejecting malformed registry shapes that json.Unmarshal accepts, specifically top-level null and null category arrays. Implemented explicit malformed handling that returns an empty map and emits a warning through the loader warning path.

/golang-code-style approved the final implementation after gofmt, go vet, go test, and make lint.

/golang-design-patterns requested caller-controlled warning output so later silent-degrade flows can use the loader without leaking stderr, plus null-shape rejection and deterministic normalized category handling. Implemented LoadCategoriesWithWarnings(libraryPath, stderr) with LoadCategories as the os.Stderr wrapper, and rejected null registry, null arrays, and trimmed duplicate categories.

/golang-linter requested resolving gosec G304 and unused helper failures. Implemented a scoped nolint:gosec rationale for reading the configured library registry and removed unused helpers from this slice. golangci-lint v2.6.2 reports 0 issues.

/golang-safety requested eliminating nondeterministic category-key collisions caused by trimming map keys during randomized map iteration. Implemented sorted raw-key traversal and treat trimmed duplicates as malformed.

/golang-testing approved coverage for happy path, missing registry, malformed JSON, null registry, null category arrays, trimmed duplicate categories, and deterministic duplicate-skill reverse lookup.

Conflict resolution:
- Foundation wording says missing/malformed should warn, while downstream tasks require absent registry to degrade silently in some contexts. Chosen path: keep LoadCategories as the warning-to-stderr API required by this bead, and add LoadCategoriesWithWarnings so downstream commands can pass nil or their configured stderr as appropriate. This preserves the public task contract and avoids forcing future CLI call sites to leak global stderr.
- For normalized category collisions, merging would preserve more data but could hide ambiguous registry ownership. Chosen path: treat collisions as malformed and return empty with a warning, matching the explicit malformed-registry fallback contract.

Verification after resolved findings:
- go test ./internal/instill -run TestLoadCategories|TestCategoryForSkill passed (8 tests)
- go run github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint@v2.6.2 run ./... passed with 0 issues
- six required re-review agents approved: /golang-cli, /golang-code-style, /golang-design-patterns, /golang-linter, /golang-safety, /golang-testing
close_reason: Implemented category registry loader, caller-controlled warning path, deterministic reverse lookup, malformed-shape handling, tests, review notes, and verification.

title: Integration tests + CI (BATS + GitHub Actions + goreleaser)
description:
End-to-end BATS test suite, GitHub Actions CI workflow, and goreleaser distribution config covering all instill commands.

## What to build

A complete quality gate and distribution pipeline:

1. **BATS test suite** — functional tests for each command: check-skills (happy path, orphan removal, broken removal, create missing), init-project (normal, --force, --skills, git warning), show-library (in/out of project, --filter), pick-skills (add, remove, all-or-nothing validation), add-hooks (add, idempotent, no-TTY no-op). Test error exits (1, 2, 3).
2. **GitHub Actions** — on push/PR: go build, go vet, golangci-lint, BATS suite against a fixture library. Build matrix for darwin/linux/amd64/arm64.
3. **goreleaser** — .goreleaser.yaml for multi-platform binary releases. Makefile with build, test, lint, install targets.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] BATS tests cover happy path for all five commands
- [ ] BATS tests cover all documented error exit codes (1, 2, 3)
- [ ] CI runs on push and PR to main
- [ ] CI builds and vets for darwin and linux, amd64 and arm64
- [ ] goreleaser produces binaries for darwin-amd64, darwin-arm64, linux-amd64, linux-arm64
- [ ] make build, make test, make install all work
- [ ] All tests pass on a clean checkout with only SKILL_LIBRARY_PATH set

## Blocked by

instill-mat, instill-d6p, instill-hs0, instill-qu4, instill-dyt, instill-rp6
notes:
Review closure for integration tests + CI:

/golang-cli requested BATS coverage for pick-skills no-args non-TTY, separated stdout/stderr assertions for representative exit 1/2/3 cases, and a make test path that works on clean checkout without silently skipping BATS. Implemented pick-skills no-args non-TTY BATS coverage, added stderr separation checks using bash -c redirection, and made make test run installed bats or pinned npx bats@1.12.0.

/golang-code-style requested required BATS, pinned golangci-lint, and a meaningful PREFIX install target. Implemented pinned GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION=v2.6.2, CI now calls make lint, make test no longer silently skips BATS, and make install writes the binary under the configured prefix bin directory.

/golang-design-patterns requested required BATS, pinned tooling, CI release validation, no mutating GoReleaser tidy hook, and BATS coverage for init-project --force. Implemented all: GoReleaser no longer mutates the tree; CI runs goreleaser check pinned to v2.15.4; BATS covers --force non-TTY preflight behavior.

/golang-linter approved; its noted BATS-skip tradeoff was resolved by the later Makefile change. Current validation uses pinned golangci-lint v2.6.2 and GoReleaser v2.15.4.

/golang-safety requested safe clean behavior, corrected --force BATS expectation, and symlinked settings.json protection. Implemented clean as a fixed repo-local artifact removal target, updated --force BATS to assert manifest remains unchanged under non-TTY preflight, and changed add-hooks to Lstat/reject symlinked settings.json with unit coverage.

/golang-testing requested required BATS and BATS coverage for init-project --force. Implemented those. It also requested normal TTY/TUI happy-path functional coverage; chosen path is not to add brittle full interactive keystroke BATS. The deterministic Go model and CLI injection tests cover normal TUI launch/confirm/cancel/large-list behavior, while BATS covers binary-level non-TTY safety and command behavior.

Conflict resolution:
- make test dependency: failing without BATS was strict but made local clean checkout less smooth; skipping was false green. Chosen path: use installed bats if present, otherwise pinned npx bats@1.12.0 when npm/npx exists, and fail only when neither runner is available.
- init-project --force under non-TTY: one earlier BATS assertion expected overwrite before failure, but safety requires TTY preflight before writes. Chosen path: keep non-TTY no-write behavior and assert the existing manifest remains unchanged.
- TTY happy-path BATS: reviewers noted platform sensitivity. Chosen path: keep interactive key handling in Go tests and binary-level TUI safety in BATS for reproducibility.

Verification after resolved findings:
- go test ./... passed (75 tests)
- go test -race ./... passed
- go vet ./... passed
- make lint passed with golangci-lint v2.6.2
- make build passed
- make test passed, running 17 BATS tests via pinned npx bats@1.12.0
- make install PREFIX=/tmp/instill-prefix-final passed
- goreleaser v2.15.4 check passed
- go mod tidy -diff clean
- darwin/linux amd64/arm64 go vet and go build passed
- make clean with an overridden binary name removed only the fixed repo-local artifacts
close_reason: Implemented BATS integration tests, CI workflow, GoReleaser config, Makefile targets, fixed review findings, and verified all gates including cross-builds.

title: add-hooks command
description:
Implements instill add-hooks to inject the instill check-skills SessionStart hook into .claude/settings.json.

## What to build

instill add-hooks merges a SessionStart hook entry into .claude/settings.json so that instill check-skills runs automatically at the start of each Claude Code session.

- No TTY: exit 0 silently (hooks are dev-time, not CI concern).
- Requires manifest; exits 1 if absent.
- Read .claude/settings.json (create empty {} if absent).
- Check if a SessionStart hook with command 'instill check-skills' already exists.
- If present: print 'already configured' and exit 0.
- If absent: merge new hook entry idempotently into the hooks.SessionStart array, write atomically.
- Print 'added SessionStart hook: instill check-skills' on success.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Exits 0 silently when no TTY available
- [ ] Exits 1 with correct message if no manifest found
- [ ] Creates .claude/settings.json if absent
- [ ] Adds SessionStart hook with command 'instill check-skills' and type 'command' under matcher ''
- [ ] Idempotent: prints 'already configured' and exits 0 if hook already present
- [ ] Does not duplicate hook entries on repeated runs
- [ ] Writes settings.json atomically
- [ ] Preserves existing settings.json content

## Blocked by

instill-d6p
notes:
Review closure for add-hooks:

/golang-cli requested preserving nested hook data, command-only idempotency, manifest parsing in TTY mode, and clarity on the no-TTY exception. Implemented raw JSON tree mutation for settings, changed idempotency to detect existing command regardless of type, parsed the manifest before settings edits when stdin is a TTY, and kept no-TTY as an intentional command-specific no-op before manifest/config work per this bead.

/golang-code-style approved with no requested changes. The missing-manifest wording note was addressed by matching SPEC.md exactly: error: no manifest found — run 'instill init-project' first.

/golang-design-patterns requested raw JSON preservation and merging into an existing empty SessionStart matcher instead of appending a duplicate matcher. Implemented both to preserve the Claude settings ownership boundary and avoid matcher order ambiguity.

/golang-linter approved with no requested changes after go test, go vet, and golangci-lint.

/golang-safety requested preserving nested hooks, preserving existing settings.json mode, and exact missing-manifest wording. Implemented raw JSON preservation, mode preservation with 0644 only for newly-created settings, and exact message alignment.

/golang-testing requested exact hook-shape assertions, TTY missing-manifest coverage, nested hook preservation coverage, and merge-preservation cases. Added tests for exact matcher/hook shape, simulated TTY no-manifest behavior, malformed manifest in TTY mode, preservation of nested hook fields/events, mode preservation, and command-only idempotency.

Conflict resolution:
- No-TTY add-hooks skips manifest/config work even though SPEC.md has global command pre-run rules. Chosen path: preserve the bead-specific no-TTY no-op so automation and noninteractive shells stay quiet; enforce manifest validation for TTY runs before mutating settings.
- Atomic writes use the shared unique-temp atomic writer rather than a literal fixed .tmp path. Chosen path: keep unique temp files because they are safer under concurrent processes while preserving atomic same-directory replacement semantics.

Verification after resolved findings:
- go test ./... passed (62 tests)
- go test -race ./... passed
- go vet ./... passed
- golangci-lint v2 run passed with 0 issues
- go build passed
close_reason: Implemented add-hooks with TTY gating, manifest validation, raw JSON settings preservation, idempotency, mode preservation, and reviewer-requested tests; all Go gates pass.

title: pick-skills TUI (interactive skill selector)
description:
Implements the Bubbletea TUI for interactive skill selection, used by pick-skills (no args) and init-project (no --skills flag).

## What to build

An interactive terminal UI using Bubbletea that allows the user to browse and toggle skills:

- Pre-populate the list from the library; check skills already in manifest.
- Space bar to toggle selection.
- / key to activate fuzzy search filter.
- Confirm (Enter) computes diff (added, removed), writes manifest atomically, runs reconciler.
- Escape/q cancels without changes.
- Used from two entrypoints: pick-skills with no args, and init-project with no --skills flag (replacing the placeholder 'use pick-skills' message from earlier slices).

Print diff output on confirm: 'added: <name>', 'removed: <name>', 'manifest: N skills'.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] TUI launches from 'instill pick-skills' (no args) when manifest exists
- [ ] TUI launches from 'instill init-project' (no --skills flag) after project files are created
- [ ] Skills pre-checked according to current manifest
- [ ] Space toggles a skill on/off
- [ ] / activates fuzzy search; typing filters the list
- [ ] Enter confirms, writes manifest atomically, runs reconciler, prints diff output
- [ ] Escape/q cancels without modifying manifest
- [ ] Works correctly with large libraries (200+ skills)

## Blocked by

instill-d6p, instill-qu4
notes:
Review closure for pick-skills TUI:

/golang-cli requested that init-project --force without --skills launch the TUI instead of silently resetting to empty. Implemented force as overwrite permission only; all no---skills init flows now call the skill picker after project files are created. Added CLI coverage for --force launching the picker.

/golang-code-style requested go mod tidy because Bubbletea was marked indirect and go.sum lacked tidy checksums. Ran go mod tidy; go mod tidy -diff is now clean.

/golang-design-patterns requested handling stale manifest skills that are no longer in the library and the same --force TUI correction. Implemented library-backed initial TUI selection so invisible stale entries are not retained, and ApplySkillSelection removes stale manifest entries on confirm with diff output. Added regression tests for both stale selection and stale removal.

/golang-linter approved with no requested changes after gofmt, go test, go vet, and golangci-lint.

/golang-safety requested preflighting TTY availability before init-project writes files when it would launch the TUI. Implemented CLI TTY preflight for no---skills init flows while preserving the existing manifest already exists error path. Added a regression test proving non-TTY init exits 2 and leaves no .claude or .gitignore behind.

/golang-testing requested real RunPickSkillsTUI boundary coverage, q cancel coverage, toggle-off coverage, and reconciling diff-output spacing. Added non-TTY RunPickSkillsTUI no-change coverage, q cancel coverage, prechecked toggle-off coverage, and kept padded diff output intentionally.

Conflict resolution:
- Diff output spacing: the bead prose says added: <name>, while SPEC.md examples and existing arg mode use padded added:   <name>. Chosen path: keep padded output for consistency with SPEC.md and existing command output; documented this in the task notes.
- Stale manifest cleanup before TUI: reviewers suggested either pre-reconcile before launch or filter initial selection and remove stale entries on confirm. Chosen path: filter initial selection and remove on confirm so the TUI stays quiet until the user confirms, then prints the explicit removed: line.
- init-project --force empty reset: current behavior was convenient, but SPEC.md says only --skills skips the TUI. Chosen path: force only authorizes overwrite; no---skills still launches TUI.

Verification after resolved findings:
- go test ./... passed (74 tests)
- go test -race ./... passed
- go vet ./... passed
- golangci-lint v2 run passed with 0 issues
- go build passed
- go mod tidy -diff clean
close_reason: Implemented Bubbletea pick-skills TUI, no-arg command launch, init-project launch including --force, non-TTY safety, stale manifest cleanup, and review-requested tests; all Go gates pass.

title: show-library command
description:
Implements instill show-library with optional project-state annotations and --filter flag.

## What to build

instill show-library reads the library directory, lists all skills (subdirs containing SKILL.md), sorted alphabetically, and prints them.

- Inside a project (manifest present): prefix each skill with [✓] if selected, [ ] if not.
- Outside a project: plain list, no annotations.
- --filter <substring>: case-insensitive substring match on skill name, applied after sorting.
- Footer line: 'N skills  (M selected)' inside project, 'N skills' outside.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Lists all library subdirectories that contain SKILL.md, sorted alphabetically
- [ ] Annotates with [✓]/[ ] when run inside a project with a manifest
- [ ] Prints plain list with no annotations when run outside a project
- [ ] --filter performs case-insensitive substring match on skill name
- [ ] Footer shows skill count and selected count (inside project) or skill count only (outside)
- [ ] Works correctly when library is empty

## Blocked by

instill-mat
notes:
Review lanes completed for instill-hs0:
- /golang-cli: requested implicit reconciliation before listing when a manifest is present.
- /golang-code-style: requested selected footer count describe visible filtered results.
- /golang-design-patterns: requested SkillExists return filesystem errors instead of collapsing them to missing, and selected count from displayed rows.
- /golang-linter: approved; golangci/vet/test gates passed.
- /golang-safety: requested selected count from filtered visible rows; path/nil/write behavior otherwise acceptable.
- /golang-testing: requested invalid library-entry tests and exact output assertions for annotated/filtered output.

Conflict resolution:
- show-library side effects: implemented implicit reconciliation for in-project runs despite read-style command expectations. Rationale: SPEC.md defines reconciliation as global behavior when a manifest is present. To reduce noise, implicit reconciliation reports only removed skills and suppresses created/ok lines.
- Selected footer count: changed to count only selected skills visible in the filtered rendered rows. Rationale: the footer reads as a summary of the displayed result set; total project selection count would be ambiguous after filtering.
- SkillExists API: changed from bool to (bool, error). Rationale: callers need to distinguish unknown skills from unreadable library state.

Executed actions:
- Added show-library command with --filter.
- Added annotated and plain library rendering.
- Added implicit reconciliation for in-project show-library, with removal-only reporting.
- Added exact unit/CLI output tests, invalid library entry coverage, filtered selected-count coverage, and missing-library reconciliation coverage.

Validation:
- go test ./...
- go test -race ./...
- go vet ./...
- golangci-lint v2 run ./...
- go build -o instill .
close_reason: Implemented show-library with six-lane review findings resolved and validation passing.

title: pick-skills command (arg mode + --remove)
description:
Implements instill pick-skills with positional skill-name args and --remove flag for manifest management.

## What to build

instill pick-skills <name...> adds named skills to the project manifest. instill pick-skills --remove <name,...> removes them.

- Requires manifest; exits 1 if absent.
- Validation is all-or-nothing: check ALL provided names against library before changing anything. If any unknown, exit 1 with correct message; make no changes.
- Merge valid names into manifest (deduplicated, sorted).
- --remove: remove named skills from manifest (names not present are silently ignored).
- Write manifest atomically: write to .claude/skill-manifest.json.tmp then rename.
- Run reconciler after any change.
- Print 'added: <name>' and/or 'removed: <name>' for each change; 'manifest: N skills' at end.
- With no args and no --remove: launch TUI (deferred to TUI slice — exit 1 with 'no skills specified' for now).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Exits 1 with correct error if no manifest found
- [ ] Validates ALL args before making any changes; rejects entire set if any unknown
- [ ] Adds named skills to manifest (deduplicated, sorted alphabetically)
- [ ] --remove removes named skills from manifest
- [ ] Manifest write is atomic (tmp file → rename)
- [ ] Reconciler runs after change
- [ ] Correct output lines: 'added:', 'removed:', 'manifest: N skills'
- [ ] Invoking with no args and no --remove exits 1 (TUI not yet implemented)

## Blocked by

instill-mat
notes:
Review lanes completed for instill-qu4:
- /golang-cli: requested --remove library validation and noted fixed temp-name vs unique temp-name tradeoff.
- /golang-code-style: requested --remove validation and raised added: padding inconsistency.
- /golang-design-patterns: requested validation of union of add/remove names before manifest reads/writes.
- /golang-linter: approved; test/vet/golangci passed.
- /golang-safety: requested --remove validation and raised added: padding inconsistency; safety otherwise acceptable.
- /golang-testing: requested unknown remove and mixed add/remove regression coverage plus no-manifest stderr assertion.

Conflict resolution:
- --remove validation: implemented validation of the normalized union of add/remove names against the library before reading or writing the manifest. Rationale: SPEC.md and bead acceptance both say all provided names must be validated all-or-nothing.
- added: padding: kept the aligned 'added:   <name>' output. Rationale: SPEC.md examples use the aligned form, and this preserves visual alignment with 'removed:'.
- Manifest temp file name: kept unique temp files instead of literal skill-manifest.json.tmp. Rationale: prior safety review found fixed temp paths race under concurrent runs; unique temp + rename better satisfies atomicity and reproducibility even though pseudocode names a simple tmp path.

Executed actions:
- Added pick-skills command with positional add args and --remove CSV.
- Added all-or-nothing library validation for add and remove names.
- Added manifest add/remove/dedupe/sort behavior and reconciliation.
- Added package and CLI tests for add, remove, unknown add, unknown remove, mixed add/remove failure, no manifest, and no args.

Validation:
- go test ./...
- go test -race ./...
- go vet ./...
- golangci-lint v2 run ./...
- go build -o instill .
close_reason: Implemented pick-skills arg mode and --remove with six-lane review findings resolved and validation passing.

title: init-project command (non-TUI path)
description:
Implements instill init-project with --skills and --force flags, creating the manifest, symlink directory, and .gitignore entry.

## What to build

instill init-project sets up a project for instill management. It must:

1. Check for existing .claude/skill-manifest.json — exit 1 with message if found and --force not set.
2. Warn if no .git directory is present (continues, does not fail).
3. Create .claude/ directory if absent.
4. Write .claude/skill-manifest.json with {"skills": []}.
5. Create .claude/skills/ directory if absent.
6. Inject into .gitignore (create if absent): a comment line and .claude/skills/ — only if not already present.
7. If --skills provided: validate all names against library (all-or-nothing — reject entire set if any unknown), write manifest, reconcile symlinks, exit.
8. If no --skills: launch pick-skills TUI (deferred to TUI slice — for now, just print 'use pick-skills to add skills' and exit 0).

Print structured output lines: 'initialized:', 'created:', 'updated:', 'warning:' as shown in the spec.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Creates .claude/skill-manifest.json with empty skills array when none exist
- [ ] Exits 1 with correct message if manifest already exists and --force not set
- [ ] --force overwrites existing manifest
- [ ] Creates .claude/skills/ directory
- [ ] Creates .gitignore if absent; appends .claude/skills/ entry with comment if not present
- [ ] --skills flag validates all names all-or-nothing; rejects with correct error if any unknown
- [ ] --skills flag writes manifest and reconciles symlinks on success
- [ ] Prints warning if not a git repo; continues without error
- [ ] Prints structured output lines matching spec

## Blocked by

instill-mat
notes:
Review lanes completed for instill-d6p:
- /golang-cli: requested exact structured output and .gitignore text, plus pre-write --skills validation for all-or-nothing behavior.
- /golang-code-style: requested wrapping long error/write calls.
- /golang-design-patterns: requested avoiding durable side effects on invalid --skills, exact warning output, and CLI boundary tests.
- /golang-linter: requested v2 golangci config fix, checked output writes, gosec/revive handling, and unused parameter cleanup.
- /golang-safety: requested .gitignore symlink protection, force-empty reconciliation to clear stale symlinks, and CRLF/anchored .gitignore idempotency.
- /golang-testing: requested exact output assertions, CLI tests for init-project flags/errors, --skills atomicity decision, and .gitignore idempotency coverage.

Conflict resolution:
- --skills all-or-nothing vs SPEC step order: chose pre-write validation and full command atomicity for --skills. Rationale: the bead acceptance says --skills validation is all-or-nothing, and this prevents data loss under --force --skills docker,missing. The SPEC step order is implementation guidance, but the durable safety contract is stronger.
- .gitignore symlink behavior: reject symlinked .gitignore with exit 3 instead of following it. Rationale: following user symlinks can write outside the project root; refusing is deterministic and safer.
- Revive package/export comments: configured revive to ignore package-comments/exported comments for this small internal CLI while keeping errcheck/gosec/staticcheck/govet active. Rationale: avoids low-signal comment churn while preserving behavioral lint coverage.
- Gosec test warnings: excluded gosec for _test.go fixtures. Rationale: temp-dir fixture permissions and variable test paths are not product risk; production gosec findings remain active with targeted justifications.

Executed actions:
- Added init-project command with --skills and --force.
- Added InitProject implementation for manifest, .claude/skills, .gitignore, warning, and reconciliation.
- Validated --skills before project writes and preserved existing manifests on invalid forced init.
- Matched spec warning/comment text and output ordering.
- Reconciled stale symlinks on --force empty reinit.
- Added .gitignore symlink rejection and CRLF/anchored idempotency handling.
- Added CLI and package tests for success, --force, existing manifest, invalid skills, warning output, idempotent .gitignore, and symlinked .gitignore.

Validation:
- go test ./...
- go test -race ./...
- go vet ./...
- golangci-lint v2 run ./...
- go build -o instill .
close_reason: Implemented init-project non-TUI path with six-lane review findings resolved and validation passing.

title: CLI scaffold + check-skills reconciler
description:
Foundational Go module with Cobra CLI skeleton, config resolution, and the core symlink reconciler that all other commands depend on.

## What to build

Bootstrap the Go module (cobra, go.mod), wire up the top-level CLI entry point, and implement two things end-to-end:

1. **Config resolution** — SKILL_LIBRARY_PATH env var → ~/.config/instill/config.json → TTY interactive prompt (writes config) → exit 2 with message when no TTY and no config.
2. **instill check-skills** — reads .claude/skill-manifest.json, reconciles .claude/skills/ symlinks in three passes: (a) remove orphaned symlinks not in manifest, (b) remove broken symlinks whose library target is gone and drop name from manifest, (c) create missing absolute symlinks for names in manifest. Prints removed/created lines; prints 'ok: N skills linked' at the end; silent when nothing changed. No-op exit 0 when no manifest found.

Exit codes: 0 success, 1 malformed manifest, 2 environment/config error, 3 filesystem error.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] go build ./... produces a binary named instill
- [ ] SKILL_LIBRARY_PATH env var is used when set; otherwise reads ~/.config/instill/config.json; prompts on TTY if neither present; exits 2 without TTY
- [ ] instill check-skills removes symlinks present in .claude/skills/ but absent from manifest
- [ ] instill check-skills removes broken symlinks (library target gone), removes name from manifest, prints 'removed: <name> (no longer in library)'
- [ ] instill check-skills creates missing absolute symlinks (.claude/skills/<name> -> <library_path>/<name>)
- [ ] Prints 'ok: N skills linked' on completion; silent when no changes needed
- [ ] Exits 0 silently when run outside a project (no manifest)
- [ ] Exits 1 on malformed manifest JSON
- [ ] Exits 3 on filesystem error creating or removing symlinks

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately
notes:
Review lanes completed for instill-mat:
- /golang-cli: requested spec-correct ~/.config/instill/config.json, real TTY detection, stderr prompt discipline, and explicit decision on orphan removal output.
- /golang-code-style: requested documented exported APIs, go mod tidy, flatter guard clauses, and less duplicated CLI cwd setup.
- /golang-design-patterns: requested real TTY detection, safe manifest skill-name validation, malformed manifest precedence over config resolution, and go mod tidy.
- /golang-linter: requested manifest path validation, .golangci.yml, go mod tidy, and orphan-removal output decision.
- /golang-safety: requested manifest path traversal prevention, unique temp-file atomic writes, and atomic config writes.
- /golang-testing: requested exit-code 3 coverage, CLI-level check-skills coverage, prompt/config tests, and stronger reconciler assertions.

Conflict resolution:
- Orphan symlink removal output: kept orphan removals silent except for the final ok line. Rationale: SPEC.md explicitly requires the detailed removed line for skills no longer in the library, while orphan cleanup is an internal reconciliation operation. This keeps command output focused on manifest-affecting changes and avoids surprising users with names they did not select.
- Config resolution global vs check-skills no-project behavior: kept check-skills as a no-manifest silent exception before config resolution. Rationale: SPEC.md says out-of-project check-skills exits 0 silently, which would be broken by unconditional root-level config resolution.
- PTY vs injection for testing prompts: used direct prompt helper tests plus golang.org/x/term for real runtime detection. Rationale: deterministic tests avoid OS-sensitive PTY behavior while runtime detection is still production-grade.

Executed actions:
- Added strict single-path-element skill validation for manifest/library joins.
- Added unique temp-file atomic writes for manifest/config.
- Switched config path to ~/.config/instill/config.json.
- Switched TTY detection to golang.org/x/term and prompts/config-written diagnostics to stderr.
- Ensured malformed manifests are parsed before config resolution in check-skills.
- Added .golangci.yml and ran go mod tidy.
- Added CLI/reconciler/config tests for valid linking, malformed manifest, no config exit 2, filesystem exit 3, invalid skill names, config path, and prompt helper behavior.

Validation:
- go test ./...
- go test -race ./...
- go vet ./...
- go build -o instill .
close_reason: Implemented CLI scaffold, config resolution, and check-skills reconciler with six-lane review findings resolved and validation passing.
